title: Devices and methods for suture placement
abstract: A suturing device includes a handle, an elongate body, an actuator and a needle holder. The handle includes a proximal handle end and a distal handle end. The elongate body connects with the handle and includes a proximal end portion and a distal end portion. The actuator is operable between a first operating position and a second operating position. The actuator includes a manually operated member including an operator contact surface spaced from the proximal handle end in a direction toward the distal handle end. The needle holder includes a distal end section having a distal-most tip and defines a needle passage and a distal opening adjacent the distal-most tip. The needle passage is configured to receive at least a portion of an associated needle when the actuator is in the first operating position.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A suturing device comprising: a handle including a proximal handle end and a distal handle end; an elongate body connected with the handle, the elongate body including a proximal end portion and a distal end portion; an actuator interacting with the elongate body and operable between a first operating position and a second operating position, the actuator including a manually operated member including an operator contact surface spaced from the proximal handle end in a direction toward the distal handle end; and a needle holder extending away from the distal end portion or provided as part of the distal end portion of the elongate body, the needle holder including a distal end section having a distal-most tip and defining a needle passage and a distal opening adjacent the distal-most tip, the needle passage being configured to receive at least a portion of an associated needle when the actuator is in the first operating position. 2. The suturing device of claim 1 , wherein the manually operated member is a slide button movable coaxial or parallel the longitudinal axis of the elongate body. 3. The suturing device of claim 2, wherein the slide button includes a flared section extending outwardly from the distal handle end. 4. The suturing device of claim 3, wherein the flared section includes a peripheral edge surrounding the longitudinal axis. 5. The suturing device of claim 4, wherein the peripheral edge is circular. 6. The suturing device of claim 2, wherein the actuator includes a wire and movement of the wire towards the distal opening results in movement of the associated curved needle toward the distal opening, wherein the slide button is 

 connected with the wire such that movement of the slide button results in movement of the wire. 7. The suturing device of claim 2, wherein the handle includes a slide button counter bore extending inwardly from the distal handle end toward the proximal handle end, wherein at least a portion of the slide button is received in the slide button counter bore when the actuator is in the first operating position. 8. The suturing device of claim 2, wherein the operator contact surface extends radially outwardly from an outer side surface of the handle. 9. The suturing device of claim 8, wherein the slide button is positioned between the proximal handle end and the distal handle end when the actuator is in the first operating position and the second operating position. 10. The suturing device of claim 8, wherein the actuator includes a wire and movement of the wire towards the distal opening results in movement of the associated needle toward the distal opening, wherein the slide button is connected with the wire such that movement of the slide button results in movement of the wire. 1 1 . The suturing device of claim 9, wherein the elongate body has a bayonet configuration and defines a proximal end portion longitudinal axis and a distal end portion longitudinal axis both residing in an elongate body plane, wherein a line emanating from the center of the handle through the operator contact surface is offset an internal angle between 5 degrees and 85 degrees from the elongate body plane. 12. The suturing device of claim 9, wherein the needle holder is a curved needle holder defining a curved needle passage following a curved needle passage radius, wherein a line emanating from the center of the handle through the operator contact surface is offset an internal angle between 5 degrees and 85 degrees from a plane 

 in which the curved needle passage radius resides. 13. The suturing device of claim 9, wherein the handle includes at least one slide button notch extending inwardly from the outer side surface of the handle, wherein the slide button slides within the slide button notch when the actuator is moved from the first operating position toward the second operating position. 14. The suturing device of claim 1 , wherein the manually operated member is a trigger that pivots with respect to the handle. 15. The suturing device of claim 14, wherein the elongate body has a bayonet configuration and defines a proximal end portion longitudinal axis and a distal end portion longitudinal axis both residing in an elongate body plane, wherein a line emanating from the center of the handle through the operator contact surface is offset an internal angle between 5 degrees and 85 degrees from the elongate body plane. 16. The suturing device of claim 14, wherein the needle holder is a curved needle holder defining a curved needle passage following a curved needle passage radius, wherein a line emanating from the center of the handle through the operator contact surface is offset an internal angle between 5 degrees and 85 degrees from a plane in which the curved needle passage radius resides. 17. The suturing device of claim 14, wherein the actuator includes a slider connected with the trigger such that pivotal movement of the trigger results in the slider moving coaxial or parallel with a longitudinal axis of the elongate body. 18. The suturing device of claim 17, wherein the actuator includes a flexure bearing element connecting the trigger with the slider. 19. The suturing device of claim 18, wherein the flexure bearing element is a 

 resilient living hinge. 20. The suturing device of claim 18, wherein the flexure bearing element biases the trigger radially outwardly from the longitudinal axis.
